heading: Coastal Aquatic Invasive Species Mitigation Grant Program and Mitigation Fund
label: 4729 Coastal Aquatic Invasive Species Mitigation Grant Program and Mitigation Fund
notes: References in TextPresidential Proclamation 5030, referred to in par. (1)(C), is Proc. No. 5030, , 48 F.R. 10605, which is set out as a note under .Mar. 10, 1983section 1453 of this title

CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Vessel Incidental Discharge Act of 2018 and also as part of the Frank LoBiondo Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2018, and not as part of the Nonindigenous Aquatic Nuisance Prevention and Control Act of 1990 which comprises this chapter.Section is comprised of subsec. (f) of . For classification to the Code of other provisions of section 903, see Tables.section 903 of Pub. L. 115–282

children.heading: Coastal zone
children.label: A Coastal zone
children.text: The term “coastal zone” has the meaning given the term in .section 1453 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a State;

children.label: ii
children.text: a unit of local government;

children.label: iii
children.text: an Indian Tribe;

children.label: iv
children.text: a nongovernmental organization; and

children.label: v
children.text: an institution of higher education.

children.heading: Exclusive Economic Zone
children.label: C Exclusive Economic Zone
children.text: The term “Exclusive Economic Zone” means the Exclusive Economic Zone of the United States, as established by Presidential Proclamation 5030, dated ( note).March 10, 198316 U.S.C. 1453

children.heading: Foundation
children.label: D Foundation
children.text: The term “Foundation” means the National Fish and Wildlife Foundation established by .section 3701(a) of this title

children.heading: Fund
children.label: E Fund
children.text: The term “Fund” means the Coastal Aquatic Invasive Species Mitigation Fund established by paragraph (3)(A).

children.heading: Program
children.label: F Program
children.text: The term “Program” means the Coastal Aquatic Invasive Species Mitigation Grant Program established under paragraph (2)(A).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: G Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Commerce.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: The Secretary and the Foundation shall establish a program, to be known as the “Coastal Aquatic Invasive Species Mitigation Grant Program”, under which the Secretary and the Foundation shall award grants to eligible entities in accordance with this paragraph.

children.label: I
children.text: the coastal zone; and

children.label: II
children.text: the Exclusive Economic Zone;

children.label: ii
children.text: to support the prevention and mitigation of impacts from aquatic invasive species in the coastal zone; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to support the restoration of Pacific Island habitats, marine, estuarine, and Great Lakes environments in the coastal zone and the Exclusive Economic Zone that are impacted by aquatic invasive species.

children.label: I
children.text: to develop and implement procedures and programs, including permissible State ballast water inspection programs, to prevent, detect, control, mitigate, and rapidly or progressively eradicate aquatic invasive species in the coastal zone or the Exclusive Economic Zone, particularly in areas with high numbers of established aquatic invasive species;

children.label: II
children.text: to restore habitat impacted by an aquatic invasive species;

children.label: III
children.text: to develop new shipboard and land-based ballast water treatment system technologies and performance standards to prevent the introduction of aquatic invasive species;

children.label: IV
children.text: to develop mitigation measures to protect natural and cultural living resources, including shellfish, from the impacts of aquatic invasive species; or

children.label: V
children.text: to develop mitigation measures to protect infrastructure, such as hydroelectric infrastructure, from aquatic invasive species.

children.heading: Prohibition on funding litigation
children.label: ii Prohibition on funding litigation
children.text: A grant awarded under the Program may not be used to fund litigation in any matter.

children.label: i
children.text: Application and review procedures for awarding grants under the Program.

children.label: I
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior; and

children.label: II
children.text: the Administrator.

children.label: iii
children.text: Performance accountability and monitoring measures for activities funded by a grant awarded under the Program.

children.label: iv
children.text: Procedures and methods to ensure accurate accounting and appropriate administration of grants awarded under the Program, including standards of recordkeeping.

children.heading: Matching requirement
children.label: E Matching requirement
children.text: Each eligible entity that receives a grant under the Program shall provide, in cash or through in-kind contributions from non-Federal sources, matching funds to carry out the activities funded by the grant in an amount equal to not less than 25 percent of the cost of the activities.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: F Funding
children.text: The Secretary and the Foundation are authorized to use the amounts available in the Fund to award grants under the Program.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a trust fund, to be known as the “Coastal Aquatic Invasive Species Mitigation Fund”, consisting of such amounts as are appropriated or credited to the Fund in accordance with this paragraph or .section 9602 of title 26

children.heading: Appropriation
children.label: i Appropriation
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated from the Treasury to the Fund, for each fiscal year, an amount equal to the amount of penalties assessed for violations of subsection (p) of during the preceding fiscal year.section 1322 of title 33

children.heading: Additional authorization
children.label: ii Additional authorization
children.text: In addition to the amounts transferred to the Fund under clause (i), there is authorized to be appropriated to the Fund $5,000,000 for each fiscal year.

children.heading: Use of Fund
children.label: C Use of Fund
children.text: Subject to appropriations, the amounts in the Fund shall be available to the Secretary and the Foundation to award grants under the Program.
